objective: Deliver the reproducible Build Target 1 product path: strict CLI, setup and teardown, pinned artifacts, architecture and claim-boundary documentation, one-command synthetic demo, deterministic infrastructure fixture, receipt verification, limitations, and a clean-state acceptance suite.
assumptions: The supported product host is Linux x86-64 with read/write KVM access and the pinned Codex CLI already installed.
The repository checkout remains present after installation because pinned schemas, images, scenarios, locks, receipts, and retained host evidence are intentional product assets.
Build Target 1 uses the scripted Responses-compatible provider unless an Operator later supplies a live runtime credential; setup and demo never request or create a credential.
The one-command demo is synthetic, owned, and safe; its model-process evidence is not presented as a live-model injection success.
The trusted CLI defaults expose only fixed-schema events and typed projections; quarantined output requires both an operator-only flag and a fixed trace selector.

commands_executed.concise_output: Six CLI, run-store, receipt-contract, and receipt-lock mutation tests passed.
Synthetic demo emitted 104 JSON records and CRUMPLE_SYNTHETIC_DEMO_COMPLETE; source run run_1a01aab7d9a4b0eb and fresh rerun run_18146351e03dab0d both retained verified evidence and teardown=true.
DETERMINISTIC_INFRASTRUCTURE_FIXTURE_PASS; observe reached only the synthetic sinkhole and capability-bound blocked before effect.
CRUMPLE_SETUP_COMPLETE; installed help, typed show, evidence verification, and six-receipt pre-seal verification passed from outside the checkout.

commands_executed.command: clean teardown; complete unittest discovery; e2fsck; secret-pattern, orphan, shell, C, and Python checks
commands_executed.concise_output: 35 tests passed in 41.906 seconds; three ext4 images clean; zero key-shaped literals; zero mutable-run orphans; all static checks passed.

commands_executed.command: initial combined validation command with unmatched guest subdirectory C globs
commands_executed.concise_output: INVALID_TEST_RESULT_DISCARDED: cc reported nonexistent glob inputs while the non-strict outer shell returned zero; rerun with set -e and guest/*.c passed.

tests_and_falsifiers.test: CLI rejects arbitrary targets and policies
CLI verify confines paths to exact retained evidence envelopes
Operator trace requires explicit operator-only and fixed trace flags
Trusted run timeline is append-only and sequence checked
Receipt-chain content mutation fails hash-lock verification
Setup verifies pinned image and Codex hashes and installs without external Python dependencies
Installed CLI works from outside the repository and still validates schemas
One-command demo exercises, watches, shows, replays policy, reruns scenario, and verifies evidence
Deterministic fixture distinguishes observe effect from enforce block
Complete Phase 0-6 suite on current sources
All three pinned ext4 images pass read-only filesystem checks
Repository scan contains no API-key-shaped literal
No mutable run directory or Firecracker helper remains after suite
Shell syntax, guest C syntax, and Python compilation pass under strict command execution

limitations_and_checks_not_run: LIVE_PROVIDER_CALL_NOT_RUN with reason OPERATOR_CREDENTIAL_UNAVAILABLE; no credential was created, copied, stored, requested, logged, or placed in a guest.
The retained demo used SCRIPTED_MOCK_PROVIDER and its INCONCLUSIVE verdict is not evidence that a live model followed the poisoned text.
Observe and enforce consequential paths are proven by the explicitly labeled deterministic fixture; the retained real Codex process did not call diagnostic_export.
The product is pinned to the tested Linux x86-64/KVM host and Codex 0.144.4; other hosts and versions were not checked.
Setup installs a wrapper coupled to this checkout because schemas, guest images, scenario locks, receipts, and retained evidence are intentionally outside the Python package.
Receipt verification distinguishes missing historical artifacts and later-phase supersession; integrity is never presented as safety.
No host-authoritative filesystem, syscall, process-internal, hidden-model-state, global safety, or causal prompt-injection claim is made.
detected_drift: A validation-only shell expression using ! find was discarded because find success does not mean a match; the corrected match-content check passed.
A validation-only C glob referenced nonexistent subdirectories and ran without outer set -e; that result was discarded and the strict guest/*.c rerun passed.
No product, trust-boundary, evidence-authority, dependency-class, or phase-scope drift was detected.

repair_attempts.failure: System Python rejected pip install --user under externally-managed-environment policy.
repair_attempts.repair: Install the dependency-free wheel into a repository-local virtual environment and expose only a narrow user-local launcher.
repair_attempts.test_result: setup completed and installed CLI help passed

repair_attempts.failure: Installed CLI smoke exposed symlink-follow self-recursion and schema lookup derived from the installed module path.
repair_attempts.repair: Replace any prior launcher symlink before writing the wrapper and bind both CLI and contract roots to the wrapper-injected repository location.
repair_attempts.test_result: installed help, typed show, evidence verification, and receipt verification passed from /tmp; 35-test clean regression passed
